text:
(7) The FDR appointment may be adjourned from time to time.


(8) At the conclusion of the FDR appointment, the court may make an appropriate consent order.


(9) If the court does not make an appropriate consent order as mentioned in paragraph (8), the court must give directions for the future course of the proceedings including, where appropriate—


(10) Both parties must personally attend the FDR appointment unless the court directs otherwise.


Textual Amendments


F41 Word in rule 9.17(9)(a) omitted (6.7.2020) by virtue of The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2020 (S.I. 2020/135) , rules 1 , 10(a)


F42 Word in rule 9.17(9)(b) substituted (6.7.2020) by The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2020 (S.I. 2020/135) , rules 1 , 10(b)


F43 Rule 9.17(9)(c) inserted (6.7.2020) by The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2020 (S.I. 2020/135) , rules 1 , 10(c)


### CHAPTER 5 E+W [ F44 FAST-TRACK PROCEDURE ]


Textual Amendments


F44 Pt. 9 Ch. 5 heading substituted (4.6.2018) by The Family Procedure (Amendment) Rules 2018 (S.I. 2018/440) , rules 1 , 7 (with rule 12 )


### Duties of the court and the applicant upon filing an application E+W


9.18. — [ F45 (A1) This Chapter applies where, in accordance with rule 9.9B, the fast-track procedure applies to an application for a financial remedy. ]


(1) [ F46 Where an application is issued ] —


(2) Where the applicant wishes to serve a copy of the application on the respondent and, on filing the application, so notifies the court—


(3) The date fixed under paragraph (1), or for any other subsequent hearing or appointment must not be cancelled except with the court's permission and, if cancelled, the court must immediately fix a new date.

metadata.title: Part 9 — Applications for a Financial Remedy
